content:
Key Findings

  • Wyre Council paid 1,563 unique suppliers between 2017/18 and 2025/26
  • 212 suppliers received under £1,000 each across the 8-year period
  • 729 suppliers received between £1,000 and £10,000 each
  • 20 suppliers received over £1 million each

The Long Tail of Small Suppliers

Wyre Council has paid 1,563 different suppliers over the past 8 years. While a small number of large suppliers dominate the council's spending, the majority are small suppliers receiving relatively modest amounts. This long tail of small suppliers could indicate a fragmented procurement process with potential inefficiencies.

The 212 suppliers who received under £1,000 each represent 13.6% of all suppliers. While the individual amounts may seem small, the cumulative cost of managing so many small suppliers could be significant. The administrative overhead of processing invoices, contracts, and payments for these suppliers may outweigh the benefits of maintaining such a diverse supplier base.

Supplier Size Distribution

The distribution of supplier sizes shows a clear long tail pattern. The majority of suppliers fall into the smaller categories, with 729 suppliers receiving between £1,000 and £10,000 each. This represents 46.7% of all suppliers. In contrast, only 20 suppliers received over £1 million each, accounting for just 1.3% of the total.

This distribution suggests that Wyre Council may benefit from consolidating its procurement processes. By reducing the number of small suppliers and consolidating spend with fewer, larger suppliers, the council could potentially realise significant cost savings through bulk purchasing and reduced administrative overhead.

Potential for Consolidation

Consolidating procurement could help Wyre Council streamline its operations and reduce costs. For example, the 212 suppliers who received under £1,000 each could potentially be consolidated into fewer, larger contracts. This would not only reduce administrative overhead but also allow the council to negotiate better terms with suppliers.

However, consolidation must be balanced with the need for competition and diversity in the supply chain. Ensuring a diverse supplier base can help the council mitigate risks and foster innovation. The key is to find the right balance between consolidation and maintaining a healthy supply chain.

What You Can Do

  • Submit a Freedom of Information request to Wyre Council about their procurement consolidation strategies
  • Attend council committee meetings to learn more about their procurement processes
  • Check the council's published contracts register to see who they are awarding contracts to
  • Write to your local councillor about the potential benefits of consolidating small suppliers
  • Visit aidoge.co.uk to explore the full spending data and see the distribution of suppliers yourself
This article is based on Wyre Council's published spending data covering 2017/18 to 2025/26. All figures are drawn directly from transactions published under the Local Government Transparency Code. Analysis by AI DOGE.
